_article: XXXIX.2 — rumors spread town to town, with decay and distortion. XXXI.1 — the RumorEngine is one of the 10 social engines. XXXI.3 — the stronger Chapter 1 metric requires rumors to circulate WITHOUT quest markers. XXXIX.5 — a rumor 'that has clearly been circulating for days (it has details, age, and disagreement)' is a canonical perceivable-momentum cue.
_description: Momentum schema for a Rumor trajectory entity. A rumor is not a broadcast. It is a live information trajectory with an origin, a subject, a belief intensity, a spread vector (which settlements it has reached and when), a distortion rate (details drift per retelling), and a decay (intensity fades unless re-catalyzed). When the player overhears two villagers discussing a rumor, that rumor has a real age, a real spread history, and real disagreement — because it has been circulating, not because a script fired.

_design_notes.why_this_matters: The user's canonical perceivable-momentum example: 'The player overhears two villagers discussing a rumor that has clearly been circulating for days (it has details, age, and disagreement).' A rumor without momentum is a quest log entry. A rumor WITH momentum has texture: it started somewhere, it reached this village at a specific time, it has been retold and distorted, some villagers believe it and some don't, and it will fade unless something re-catalyzes it. This is how the Heng Yue recruiter's visit, the patriarch's seclusion, the wolf attacks, or a stranger's arrival become known — through rumor trajectories, not notifications.
_design_notes.coupling: Feeds: NPC belief_registry (villagers who hear the rumor update beliefs), motivation_state (a rumor may trigger or shift an NPC's motivation). Fed by: any threshold-crossing event in any other momentum (a wolf attack seeds a rumor; a caravan arrival seeds a rumor; a patriarch's seclusion seeds a rumor). Carried by: caravan_momentum (caravans carry rumors between settlements), NPC travel.
perceivable_without_notification_example: Two villagers arguing about whether the recruiter is coming this month or next — the rumor has age and disagreement.
A villager mentions 'they say the patriarch hasn't been seen in weeks' — the seclusion rumor has reached this village.
A child tells a version of the wolf-attack story with the wrong number of wolves — the rumor has distorted in retelling.
A merchant in a neighboring village knows something that happened in Wang Village three days ago — the rumor traveled with a caravan.

current_state.subject: string — what the rumor is about (entity id or event id)
current_state.subject_type: enum — event | person | place | faction | omen | opportunity | threat
current_state.origin_tick: int — when it started (age = current_tick - origin_tick)
current_state.belief_intensity: float 0.0–1.0 — how strongly the average hearer believes it (decays over time unless re-catalyzed)
current_state.spread_vector: array of {settlement_id, first_heard_tick, local_belief_intensity 0-1, local_distortion 0-1} — which settlements have heard it, when, and how they've distorted it
current_state.distortion_rate_per_retelling: float — how much details drift per retelling (some rumor types distort fast — omens; some slow — witnessed events)
current_state.decay_rate_per_tick: float — belief_intensity fades unless re-catalyzed by a new event or a charismatic reteller
current_state.disagreement_level: float 0.0–1.0 — how much hearers disagree about the rumor's truth (high disagreement = the rumor has been retold enough to fork)

rate_of_change.spread_per_tick: float — new settlements hearing the rumor per tick (driven by caravan traffic, NPC travel, market days)
rate_of_change.distortion_per_tick: float — details drift as the rumor retells; older rumors in farther settlements distort more

components.natural_decay: float — belief fades at rumor-type-specific rate (omens fade fast; witnessed events fade slow)
components.caravan_spread: float — each caravan arrival from an infected settlement spreads the rumor (driven by caravan_momentum)
components.npc_spread: float — traveling NPCs (merchants, recruiters, wandering cultivators) spread rumors
components.re_catalysis: float — a new related event raises belief_intensity (a second wolf attack re-catalyzes the wolf rumor)
components.charismatic_reteller_boost: float — an NPC with high social standing retelling the rumor raises local belief_intensity

drivers: string — e.g. 'rumor_heng_yue_recruiter_visit: subject=heng_yue_recruiter, origin=wang_family_village, origin_tick 42000 (age 8000 ticks ~ 5 days), belief_intensity 0.72, spread_vector=[wang_family_village 0.78 belief 0.05 distortion, east_hamlet 0.61 belief 0.12 distortion (they think it is next month not this), zhao_market 0.44 belief 0.20 distortion], decay 0.02/tick, re_catalysis possible if recruiter actually arrives'

thresholds.name: regional_reach
thresholds.condition: spread_vector length >= regional_threshold AND belief_intensity > 0.5 in multiple settlements
thresholds.consequence: rumor is regionally known; NPCs across the region may reference it in ambient dialogue; it may shift NPC motivations (a parent in a neighboring village may bring a child to Wang Village hoping to catch the recruiter).

thresholds.name: distortion_fork
thresholds.consequence: rumor has forked into incompatible versions; hearers in different settlements believe different things; the original subject may be unrecognizable. This is how history becomes legend and legend becomes myth in Er Gen canon.

thresholds.name: fading
thresholds.condition: belief_intensity < 0.2 in all settlements AND no re-catalysis for sustained period
thresholds.consequence: rumor is fading; NPCs stop mentioning it; it persists only in the memory_ledger of those who heard it (and may resurface if a related event occurs). This is the natural death of a rumor.

thresholds.name: confirmed
thresholds.condition: subject event actually occurs (recruiter arrives, wolf attacks, patriarch emerges)
thresholds.consequence: rumor is confirmed in the settlements that heard it; belief_intensity spikes to ~1.0 in those settlements; the rumor transforms from speculation into remembered fact (enters memory_ledger as confirmed). Settlements that didn't hear it remain ignorant — information asymmetry.

thresholds.name: disproved
thresholds.condition: subject event fails to occur AND a counter-evidence event occurs
thresholds.consequence: rumor is disproved; belief_intensity crashes; hearers update beliefs; the rumor may transform into a 'false alarm' memory. The NPC who spread it most loudly may lose social standing (relationship_graph trust dimension).

interruptible_by: Player or NPC publicly confirms or denies (shifts belief_intensity sharply)
Subject event occurs (confirms) or definitively fails (disproves)
Authority figure (sect elder, village head) rules on the rumor (shifts belief in their influence zone)
A bigger rumor eclipses it (attention scarcity)
Re-catalysis event (a second wolf attack re-ignites the fading wolf rumor)
schema.perceivable_without_notification: string — REQUIRED (Art XXXIX.5). e.g. 'Two villagers at the well disagree about whether the Heng Yue recruiter comes this month or next — the rumor is five days old and has already forked. A merchant from the east hamlet mentions, unprompted, that he heard the patriarch of Heng Yue has not been seen — his version adds that the sect is hiding a scandal (it isn't; that's distortion). A child tells the wolf story with seven wolves, not nine.' If empty, FAILS the Momentum Test.
schema.compiled_into: feeds belief_registry (NPC beliefs), motivation_state (rumor-triggered motivations); carried by travel_graph (caravan/NPC spread)
schema.canon_ref: EXPLICIT — rumors, their spread, and their distortion are pervasive Er Gen canon (every sect has rumors; every Wang Lin act spawns legends that distort)
